prevalence.population: Women of reproductive age in Brazil

vulnerablePopulations: women with limited access to gynecologic care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Amsel criteria (3/4) or Nugent score on Gram stain; vaginal pH >4.5 typical; differentiate from VVC and trichomoniasis

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no routine screening; pregnancy screening varies by context

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often clinical diagnosis; microscopy/Gram stain availability varies
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Metronidazole or clindamycin regimens; counsel to avoid douching and manage recurrence; pregnancy management per guidelines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High recurrence and limited lab confirmation; overlap with STIs and limited access to follow-up
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective low-cost antibiotic treatments are widely available
